message:
fix(fcp-oauth): make OAuthTokens::from_response fallible + dedicated EmptyTokenField error (br-1mmu9)

Close flywheel_connectors-1mmu9. The prior hardening in 6a20d755 moved
the failure point for empty access_token/token_type from the wire-level
constructor to the authorization_header() call-site, but
OAuthTokens::from_response remained infallible — it would build a
structurally invalid OAuthTokens with "" access_token and happily put it
into the TokenStore. Downstream callers discovered the invariant
violation only when they called authorization_header(), by which point
the bad token had already entered persistence / caches and could
pollute refresh loops.

Fix: enforce the invariant at the construction boundary.

## New dedicated error variant

crates/fcp-oauth/src/error.rs

Add OAuthError::EmptyTokenField(&'static str):

  /// OAuth token field was present but empty.
  #[error("OAuth token field cannot be empty: {0}")]
  EmptyTokenField(&'static str),

Splitting this out of the InvalidTokenResponse(String) bucket lets
callers match on the specific invariant violation and lets the compiler
check `matches!(err, OAuthError::EmptyTokenField("access_token"))`
without depending on the exact error message. The payload is a
&'static str (one of "access_token" or "token_type") so construction is
zero-allocation — useful since the error is produced inline inside a
token refresh loop.

## Validator on TokenResponse

crates/fcp-oauth/src/token.rs

Add TokenResponse::validate(self) -> OAuthResult<Self>. Returns self
unchanged on success so callers can chain
`TokenResponse.validate()?.into_whatever()`. Rejects empty access_token
with EmptyTokenField("access_token"), empty token_type with
EmptyTokenField("token_type"). Encapsulating the invariant on
TokenResponse means NOBODY can construct an OAuthTokens from a bad
response — the guard sits at the bottleneck.

## from_response is now fallible

Change signature from
  pub fn from_response(response: TokenResponse) -> Self
to
  pub fn from_response(response: TokenResponse) -> OAuthResult<Self>

First line inside the function: `let response = response.validate()?;`
so every path to OAuthTokens construction goes through the validator.
Drop the now-incorrect `#[must_use]` attribute — Result is inherently
must-use.

## authorization_header + update_from_response: swap error variants

Both call sites that previously returned
OAuthError::InvalidTokenResponse("... empty access_token".into()) and
"... empty token_type".into() now return
EmptyTokenField("access_token") / EmptyTokenField("token_type").
Consistent error taxonomy — every "field is empty" failure flows
through the same variant regardless of which constructor or mutator
surfaced it. Doc comments updated to point at EmptyTokenField.

## oauth2.rs: delete the duplicate check

crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs

The OAuth2Client::exchange_code path had its own in-line access_token
and token_type emptiness checks that returned
OAuthError::InvalidTokenResponse("server returned empty access_token"
.into()) before calling OAuthTokens::from_response. Replace the two
if-empty branches with direct EmptyTokenField returns (for the benefit
of callers that match on the typed variant), and propagate the now-
fallible from_response with `?`. Net effect: the same invariant is
checked twice (once in oauth2.rs before from_response and once inside
from_response via validate), but the second check is cheap and keeps
from_response's contract self-contained for callers that bypass the
OAuth2Client.

## Test updates across three files

All test sites that previously did
  let tokens = OAuthTokens::from_response(resp);
now either call a tiny helper
  fn valid_tokens(response: TokenResponse) -> OAuthTokens {
      OAuthTokens::from_response(response)
          .expect("valid token fixture must construct")
  }
or inline the `.expect(...)`. Two new unit tests in token.rs explicitly
pin the TokenResponse::validate() contract:
  - test_token_response_validate_rejects_empty_access_token
  - test_token_response_validate_rejects_empty_token_type
The matches! assertions in the update_from_response regression tests
are strengthened to match the specific EmptyTokenField(&'static str)
payload, not just the erased InvalidTokenResponse(_) bucket.

## Beads

Update flywheel_connectors-1mmu9 timestamp (stays in_progress — the
closure bead will land in the next sweep after verification).
Close flywheel_connectors-b57hn with reason "Fixed overflow-safe
genesis fingerprint hashing and added a post-2262 regression test."
pointing at the prior commit 88d515b7 in this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -60,6 +60,10 @@ pub enum OAuthError {
     #[error("Invalid token response: {0}")]
     InvalidTokenResponse(String),
 
+    /// OAuth token field was present but empty.
+    #[error("OAuth token field cannot be empty: {0}")]
+    EmptyTokenField(&'static str),
+
     /// HTTP request failed.
     #[error("HTTP request failed: {0}")]
     HttpError(String),
